body.title: CLOSED: CC auto-memory approach-frame sweep
body.result: Scanned 4 feedback_*.md files; reframed 2: orchestration (don't-assume negatives → shape-question positive), igor-tickets (don't-count → confirm-before-counting). 2 files clean.
body.description:
CC's auto-memory directory (~27 feedback_*.md and project_*.md files) contains rule-shaped guidance including avoidance-frame language ("don't write to...", "never...", "must not"). Parallel to T-igor-self-audit-approach-frame which scans Igor's clan.memories for avoidance-frame instructions: this ticket scans CC's file-based feedback corpus, proposes reframes or deletions per file (based on superseded status, obsolescence, or purity-as-safety-bit-now-in-skill), and surfaces candidates to Akien for per-file decision.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/projects/-home-akien-TheIgors/memory/ (feedback_*.md and project_*.md, excluding MEMORY.md index)

**Design rules:** none apply (file I/O only, no new schemas, no code changes, no memory shape mutations)

**Scope boundary:** IN — scan feedback_*.md + project_*.md for avoidance-frame markers ("don't", "never", "must not", "avoid", "stop"), propose per-file action (reframe-text suggestion OR delete + rationale). OUT — actually applying reframes (Akien decision step); restructuring memory organization.

**Test plan:** Fixture: 3 synthetic feedback files (pure-avoidance, mixed, approach-framed). Verify: pure-avoidance flagged; mixed flagged with excerpt; approach-framed passes. Integration: run scan against real directory, spot-check detection of existing avoidance-frame patterns (e.g., "don't write to decisions_log.dsb" from feedback_persist_in_tickets_or_slate.md which we just deleted).

Drain UX: /day-close + slate surface (matching T-igor-self-audit-approach-frame). Worker: igor. Trigger: manual deletion of feedback_persist_in_tickets_or_slate.md (2026-04-29) surfaced that avoidance-frame feedback is a systemic pattern across CC's memory directory.
